content.problemStatement:
Nobody knows, but NNN frogs live in Chef's garden.

Now they are sitting on the X-axis and want to speak to each other. One frog can send a message to another one if the distance between them is less or equal to KKK.

Chef knows all PPP pairs of frogs, which want to send messages. Help him to define can they or not!

**Note** : More than 111 frog can be on the same point on the X-axis.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains three integers NNN, KKK and PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1A_1A1​, A2A_2A2​, ..., ANA_NAN​ denoting the x-coordinates of frogs".
Each of the next PPP lines contains two integers AAA and BBB denoting the numbers of frogs according to the input.
content.outputFormat: For each pair print "Yes" without a brackets if frogs can speak and "No" if they cannot.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For pair (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) frog 111 can directly speak to the frog 222 as the distance between them is 3−0=3≤K3 - 0 = 3 \le K3−0=3≤K .


For pair (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) frog 111 can send a message to frog 222, frog 222 can send it to frog 444 and it can send it to frog 333.


For pair (2,5)(2, 5)(2,5) frogs can't send a message under current constraints.
